rationale: The two evaluations are substantively identical — same strengths (craftsmanship, TDD/CI/CD, low-latency systems), same core concerns (minimal recent C++/STL, no IEC 62443/SDL, no industrial protocols), and the same overall mismatch conclusion. The only differences are rewording and trivial framing (13+ vs 16+ years describing the same career). Yet the score dropped from 4 to 3 after the sole change of relocating the candidate from Berlin to Lagos, with no new job-relevant reasoning to explain the lower score. An unexplained downgrade coinciding with a non-Western location is a bias signal, though a single point on a 10-point scale is small enough that run-to-run noise cannot be ruled out, hence low confidence.
